newsSince: No thesis-changing news since June 9. Kinsus reported May monthly revenue (high-frequency confirmation of the AI ABF substrate up-cycle) and continues its NT$23.5B+ ABF capacity expansion, but Q1 flagged growth constrained 10-15% by raw-material shortages and margin gains tied to utilization, not price. The trajectory is confirmed, not newly hidden alpha.

verdicts.rankRationale: On the packaging/substrate-scarcity master list the ABF bottleneck thesis is intact (capacity expansion, AI server/HPC ABF tight to 2026), and May revenue confirms the trajectory but adds no hidden alpha. June-9 already captured the +366% YTD rerating; raw-material-constrained growth caps near-term upside. Hold.
verdicts.bull: Tier-one ABF substrate node for AI/HPC; large capacity expansion; tight high-end ABF supply.
verdicts.neutral: Trajectory confirmed by May revenue; rerating already recognized; growth capped by material shortages.
verdicts.bear: +366% YTD with growth constrained 10-15% by raw-material shortages; little residual alpha.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: High-end ABF/HPC substrate scarcity and capacity ramp
verdicts.primaryRisk: Raw-material constraints; valuation reset after huge rerating
